NCT01206062_SENIOR.name: SPRINT-SENIOR
NCT01206062_SENIOR.group: Systolic Target ≥75y subgroup. SPRINT was a single RCT (NCT01206062) pre-specified into age-stratified subgroups; SENIOR (n=2,636) and CKD (n=2,646) subgroups share neither patients nor randomization with each other but DO depend on the parent SPRINT design. Pooled meta-analysis treating these as independent will under-estimate variance ~5-10% via shared methodology effects. Recommended: subgroup-only or sensitivity analysis with joint variance.

allOutcomes.title: Primary CV Composite (MI, ACS, Stroke, HF, CV Death)

evidence.label: Primary Result (Senior Subgroup)
evidence.text: Intensive treatment (target <120) significantly reduced MACE (102 vs 148, HR 0.66) and mortality (73 vs 107, HR 0.67) in seniors.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)

NCT01206062_CKD.name: SPRINT-CKD
NCT01206062_CKD.group: Systolic Target CKD subgroup. Sister-subgroup of SPRINT-SENIOR — same parent trial NCT01206062 with different stratification slice. See SPRINT-SENIOR group field for dependency note.

evidence.label: Primary Result (CKD Subgroup)
evidence.text: In CKD patients, intensive SBP targeting reduced primary composite (112 vs 131, HR 0.81) and all-cause death (70 vs 95, HR 0.72).

evidence.label: Primary Result
evidence.text: Targeting SBP <120 did not significantly reduce MACE (208 vs 237, HR 0.88) but reduced stroke (36 vs 62, HR 0.59).

evidence.label: Primary Result
evidence.text: Intensive target (110-130) reduced primary composite (147 vs 196, HR 0.74) and stroke (48 vs 71, HR 0.67).

evidence.text: Intensive SBP target (<130) non-significantly reduced recurrent stroke (125 vs 152, HR 0.81).
